name: Snow Report 2026-03-15 16:02:17
report:
Good afternoon, Beast fans, 

False spring took a break this weekend, as 8” of snow in the last 48 hours allowed us to re-open a number of trails throughout today. Tomorrow, the weather is taking a turn for the worse, but there is light at the end of the tunnel: snowmaking will be back on Tuesday! More on that to come. 

As for tomorrow, a not-so-fun weather system will be greeting us overnight, bringing warmer temperatures, increased winds, and underdeveloped snowflakes. Temps will teeter right around freezing as the initial precipitation comes in, so there might be a bit of snow at higher elevations during the night. The incoming warm-up will hopefully give our snow surfaces some give first thing in the morning, but the precipitation looks to continue in a warmer fashion through the morning as well. Temps will rise in the afternoon to reach about 50° by the time lifts close. 

Snow conditions today were great! The extra 3” overnight brought on some additional ungroomed terrain for those up for the challenge. I got to make some fresh tracks on Skyehawk in the afternoon, which was pretty gnarly (in a fun way). Our groomers will be tasked with maintaining our snow surfaces through this upcoming weather, but they’ll keep doing what they do best: getting the best possible product out there for skiing & riding. Surfaces will definitely become spring-y again by tomorrow afternoon if you prefer a softer snow underfoot. 

On the grooming front, our team will tackle around 80 trails overnight, and those would be the safest to stick to right at opening tomorrow. Downdraft and Cascade will see a winch grooming overnight if you prefer the steeps. For a nice cruise, Upper Pipe Dream is always a dreamy trek. Cruise Control had a great surface today, even as the fresh snow got kicked around by the afternoon. See our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of groomed runs. 

We have 15 lifts scheduled to spin tomorrow, but do check that Lifts & Trails Report for any potential wind delays. Southward winds may have an effect on some of our non-fixed-grip lifts during the day. 

Back to the good news: our snowmakers will be out in force this Tuesday, as The Beast’s snowguns will be roaring to life once again. We’ll be laying down coverage in key areas around the mountain, from Bear to Snowdon and plenty in between. Check back here tomorrow for more specifics on which trails will be receiving some love. 

Up in our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard Parks offering beginner-friendly features for all. Timberline remains jam-packed with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. At Bear Mountain, Dream Maker continues to offer big air and big thrills with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base of Bear. The Stash brings along fun natural features to our lengthy feature list, as well as a couple more jumps. Check out our Terrain Parks Report for full details. 

Finally, if you can’t get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass at any time for just $199. 

Have a great week! 

- Cullen 

  

Operating Day: 125 

Lifts         

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.        

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.         

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!         

The Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open Friday - Sunday through March 22.    

Uphill Travel         

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.      

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. name: Snow Report 2026-03-15 05:42:32
report:
Good morning, Beast fans!

Winter is back in full swing today as The Beast has become a winter wonderland once again. The mountain as covered in 8” of powder from the past 48 hours, with an additional 3" coming in last night. With Friday night's snow getting packed down by our groomers and last night's snow adding more fluff on top, today should be a fun one out there.

Today will bring more cloud coverage overhead, with temperatures topping out around 30 at the base and 24 at the peak. Expect breezy conditions throughout the afternoon as winds shift and pick up before lifts stop for the evening.

While surface conditions were variable at times yesterday, there was plenty of packed powder and pow pockets to snag. The mountain has seen a huge improvement in snow surfaces since the recent refreeze, and today should be smoother than yesterday as the new snow gets worked in and hard surfaces get softened up. Yesterday, the Canyon had goods in Double Dipper and Downdraft with powdery turns, and I wouldn't be surprised to find more there today.

For groomers, our team added Ovation to the 82 trails that will get a refresh tonight. These are the kinds of days where our Mountain Ops teams work their magic to smooth out any variable conditions out there. In the morning, groomers should be skiing and riding smoothly out there. Bunny Buster should be good option today, and you can’t go wrong with cruisers on Snowdon as most of those runs get regroomed. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report before booting up to get the full rundown. 

Our key lifts are scheduled for 8:00 a.m. openings today, with the Canyon Quad and South Ridge Quad joining the fun at 9:00 a.m. Get those South Ridge laps in while you can! That's where I'll end up sometime this morning.

The forecast is showing some friendly snowmaking temperatures starting on St. Patrick’s Day, so you know what that means: our crew is planning to fire up the snowguns once again, laying down extra support around the mountain to prep snow surfaces even further for the spring. name: Snow Report 2026-03-14 13:15:20
report:
Good morning, Beast fam!

It seems like this March is finally delivering the goods as 5" of fresh snow has dropped down on us since last night. Another night of grooming got our snow surfaces back on track yesterday, and a fun day of packed powder is in the cards for us. 

Weather-wise, we’re looking at another classic wintry day on the hill. Temps will float in the high 20s at our base and in the low 20s at the peak, with westward winds picking up slightly during the day. It looks like snow will fall until around 2:00 p.m., so use caution when travelling up to the mountain. 

Snow surfaces made a nice recovery from Wednesday night’s freeze, as our Mountain Ops team worked their magic in safely re-opening many of our trails. I caught some corduroy on Blue Heaven and Lower Wildfire before eventually heading down towards Skyeship base, where Upper Home Stretch was another highlight. The peak-to-creek trek should be gorgeous today after trees are again nicely covered in the white stuff. 

If you’re looking to stick to groomers, you’re in luck: 87 of our 92 open trails are slated to get groomed before the morning. Trails that got added to the list are Lower Home Stretch and Lower Catwalk, with Frolic and Upper Great Bear also getting cord over on Snowdon. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report before booting up to get the full rundown. 

Outer Limits is also groomed, with service from the Bear Mountain Quad starting at 7:30 a.m. today. For those looking to be an early bird and get some of the first tracks, consider parking over at Bear Mountain to catch the early lift. The rest of our key lifts are scheduled for 8:00 a.m. openings, with the Canyon Quad, South Ridge Quad, and Snowdon Triple joining the fun at 9:00 a.m. 

The forecast is showing some friendly snowmaking temperatures starting on St. Patrick’s Day, so you know what that means: our crew is planning to fire up the snowguns once again, laying down extra support around the mountain to prep snow surfaces even further for the spring. Read our latest Insider Blog to learn how much our recent investments in snowmaking have worked out nicely this season. 

For our park riders or any lovers of snowboarding history, we’ll be hosting A Day for Jake today to honor the life and legacy of Jake Burton Carpenter. Rally to The Stash around 11:30 a.m. for the noontime group photo at The Stash House followed by a group ride down to Bear Mountain. 

In the rest of our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard Parks offering beginner-friendly features for all. Timberline remains jam-packed with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. At Bear Mountain, Dream Maker continues to offer big air and big thrills with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base of Bear. Check out our Terrain Parks Report for full details. 

If you can’t get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass at any time for just $199. 

Finally, the Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill is bringing a one-night-only Jamaica Carnival–inspired dinner to the Clubhouse Grill. Pre-order here by 3:00 p.m. today and pick up your meal between 6:00 and 7:30 p.m. after your Saturday on the mountain. Quantities are limited and all orders must be prepaid online with no substitutions, so get your order in while you can! 

Have a great weekend! 

- Evan

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.       

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.        

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. name: Snow Report 2026-03-14 05:55:28
report:
Good morning, Beast fam!

It seems like this March is finally going to deliver the goods as 1" of fresh snow fell last night and we're projected to see 3”-5” of powder by the end of the day. Another night of grooming got our snow surfaces back on track yesterday, and a fun day of packed powder looks to be in the cards for us. 

Weather-wise, we’re looking at another classic wintry day on the hill. Temps will float in the high 20s at our base and in the low 20s at the peak, with westward winds picking up slightly during the day. It looks like snow will fall until around 2:00 p.m., so use caution when travelling up to the mountain. 

Snow surfaces made a nice recovery from Wednesday night’s freeze, as our Mountain Ops team worked their magic in safely re-opening many of our trails. I caught some corduroy on Blue Heaven and Lower Wildfire before eventually heading down towards Skyeship base, where Upper Home Stretch was another highlight. The peak-to-creek trek should be gorgeous today after trees are again nicely covered in the white stuff. 

If you’re looking to stick to groomers, you’re in luck: 87 of our 93 open trails are slated to get groomed before the morning. Trails that got added to the list are Lower Home Stretch and Lower Catwalk, with Frolic and Upper Great Bear also getting cord over on Snowdon. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report before booting up to get the full rundown. 

Outer Limits is also groomed, with service from the Bear Mountain Quad starting at 7:30 a.m. today. For those looking to be an early bird and get some of the first tracks, consider parking over at Bear Mountain to catch the early lift. The rest of our key lifts are scheduled for 8:00 a.m. openings, with the Canyon Quad, South Ridge Quad, and Snowdon Triple joining the fun at 9:00 a.m. 

The forecast is showing some friendly snowmaking temperatures starting on St. Patrick’s Day, so you know what that means: our crew is planning to fire up the snowguns once again, laying down extra support around the mountain to prep snow surfaces even further for the spring. Read our latest Insider Blog to learn how much our recent investments in snowmaking have worked out nicely this season. 

For our park riders or any lovers of snowboarding history, we’ll be hosting A Day for Jake today to honor the life and legacy of Jake Burton Carpenter. Rally to The Stash around 11:30 a.m. for the noontime group photo at The Stash House followed by a group ride down to Bear Mountain. 

In the rest of our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard Parks offering beginner-friendly features for all. Timberline remains jam-packed with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. At Bear Mountain, Dream Maker continues to offer big air and big thrills with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base of Bear. Check out our Terrain Parks Report for full details. 

If you can’t get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass at any time for just $199. 

Finally, the Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill is bringing a one-night-only Jamaica Carnival–inspired dinner to the Clubhouse Grill. Pre-order here by 12:00 p.m. tomorrow and pick up your meal between 6:00 and 7:30 p.m. after your Saturday on the mountain. Quantities are limited and all orders must be prepaid online with no substitutions, so get your order in while you can! 

Have a great weekend! 

- Evan

We also got good news, as the storm coming in tonight has been bumped up to a Winter Weather Advisory with 5 to 9 inches forecasted once it’s all said and done. Mother Nature isn’t done bringing winter just quite yet, and that extra base will be excellent for later in the season. 

Today brings a mix of sun and clouds with a high near 29 at the base and 22 at the top. Winds shift to the south through the afternoon, and snow is likely to move in late in the day with the potential for 1 to 2 inches of fresh.

Surface conditions will continue to be variable, consisting of loose granular, machine groomed, and scattered areas of hardpack and thin cover, although we still have plenty of snow on our base. Groomers will again be the best choice for making turns. Our grooming team got to Cruise Control, Bunny Buster, and 75 other trails. Check out the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown on our terrain.

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the most recent cold stretch last week, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. We're not done making snow quite yet, as the crew will be firing back up next week. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off (spoiler alert: they’re paying off big time).

Scheduled lifts will spin at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad opening at 9:30 a.m. The South Ridge and Canyon Quads will be spinning at 10:00 a.m.

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. The Stash is also open again after last weekend’s Slash & Berm Banked Slalom and is back in play. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

Grab your 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass at any time for just $199. 

Rentals & demos for the weekend can be picked up ahead of time today between 2:00 p.m. and 6:00 p.m. 

Finally, the Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill is bringing a one-night-only Jamaica Carnival–inspired dinner to the Clubhouse Grill. Pre-order here by 12:00 p.m. on Saturday, March 14 and pick up your meal between 6:00 and 7:30 p.m. after your Saturday on the mountain. Quantities are limited and all orders must be prepaid online with no substitutions, so be sure to order early!

Let’s get after it!

 – Evan

 

Operating Day: 122

Lifts       

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.      

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.       

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!       

The Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open Friday - Sunday through March 22.  

Uphill Travel       

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.    

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.
